headline: Malaysia's PISA 2025 Results Show Widening Urban-Rural Achievement Gap
context: The latest PISA results reveal that the performance gap between urban and rural students grew by 15 points since 2022.

cards.big: Urban-rural PISA gap widened to 62 points in 2025 equivalent to 2 years of schooling
cards.sub: Rural students score at the level of urban students 2 grade levels below them

cards.big: Malaysia's overall PISA score remained at 415 well below OECD average of 487
cards.sub: Stagnation over 3 cycles suggests systemic reform is needed not incremental fixes

cards.big: Bottom 25% of Malaysian students scored 80 points below the international low benchmark
cards.sub: Significant portion of students lack minimum competency for productive adult life

cards.big: Poland closed its urban-rural PISA gap by 35 points through equalized school funding reform
cards.sub: Resource equalization was more impactful than curriculum changes alone

cards.big: National average scores hide the crisis facing rural students who fall further behind each cycle
Should PISA results trigger automatic additional funding for lowest-performing regions
